text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

--- PATIENT INFORMATION ---
Patient Name: Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo
Date of Birth: [DOB on file]
Medical Record Number: [MRN on file]
Attending Physician: Hematology-Oncology
Pathologist: [Attending Hematopathologist]

--- SPECIMEN INFORMATION ---
Specimen ID: [LAB ACCESSION NUMBER]
Specimen Type: Bone marrow core biopsy (right posterior iliac crest) and bone marrow aspirate (right posterior iliac crest)
Date of Procedure: [DATE]
Date Received in Laboratory: [DATE]
Date of Final Report: [DATE]
Clinical Indication: Leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/uL), peripheral blastosis (~42% by manual differential), anemia, thrombocytopenia, and petechiae. Rule out acute leukemia.

--- GROSS PATHOLOGY ---
Bone Marrow Core Biopsy: Received is a core biopsy measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The specimen is tan-pink, firm, and intact. Submitted entirely in one cassette after decalcification.

Bone Marrow Aspirate: Received are multiple aspirate smears (Wright-Giemsa stained, 6 slides) and a clot section. The aspirate material is adequate for differential count. Particulate marrow fragments are identifiable. Additional aliquots were submitted for flow cytometry immunophenotyping and cytogenetics as requested.

--- DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS ---

BONE MARROW CORE BIOPSY (H&E, PAS, reticulin stains):

Cellularity: Markedly hypercellular, estimated at 90-95% (age-adjusted normal for a 48-year-old female: approximately 50-60%). The adipose tissue is nearly completely replaced.

Architecture: The normal hematopoietic architecture is effaced. There is diffuse, sheet-like replacement of the marrow space by a monotonous population of large immature cells morphologically consistent with myeloblasts. Residual scattered erythroid islands and rare dysplastic megakaryocytes are identified at the periphery of the biopsy core. The sinusoidal architecture is largely preserved.

Blast Morphology: The predominant cells are large, with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, irregular to round nuclear contours, finely dispersed to vesicular chromatin, and one to three prominent nucleoli. Moderate amounts of pale to lightly basophilic cytoplasm are present. On Wright-Giemsa stained aspirate smears, thin needle-like azurophilic cytoplasmic inclusions consistent with Auer rods are identified in a subset of the blast population (estimated 10-15% of blasts; single Auer rods per cell, no faggot cells identified). No overt monocytic differentiation is appreciated by morphology.

Granulopoiesis: Markedly suppressed. Only rare maturing granulocytic forms identified.

Erythropoiesis: Markedly suppressed. Rare erythroid precursors present without significant dysplasia.

Megakaryopoiesis: Markedly decreased. Occasional dysplastic forms with hypolobated or monolobated nuclei noted, representing fewer than 5 megakaryocytes per high-power field on core.

Reticulin Stain (silver impregnation): MF-1 fibrosis (mild reticulin increase with no intersections). No significant myelofibrosis.

PAS Stain: Scattered granular PAS positivity in the blast population, pattern consistent with myeloid differentiation.

BONE MARROW ASPIRATE DIFFERENTIAL (500-cell manual differential, Wright-Giemsa):
  Myeloblasts:                     74%
  Promyelocytes:                    2%
  Myelocytes:                       1%
  Metamyelocytes/bands/segs:        3%
  Monocytes/promonocytes:           1%
  Eosinophils:                      0%
  Basophils:                        0%
  Erythroblasts (all stages):       9%
  Lymphocytes:                      8%
  Plasma cells:                     1%
  Other/unclassifiable:             1%

Aspirate adequacy: Adequate. Multiple spicules present.
Auer rods confirmed on aspirate smears in a subset of blasts.

--- ANCILLARY STUDIES ---

FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (10-color multiparameter flow, fresh aspirate):
Gating strategy: CD45 dim/low SSC gate used to identify blast population. An aberrant CD34+/CD117+ population constitutes approximately 72% of all nucleated marrow events.

Blast Population Immunophenotype:
  POSITIVE (bright/moderate expression): CD34, CD117 (c-Kit), CD13, CD33, HLA-DR, MPO (cytoplasmic)
  POSITIVE (dim expression): CD123, CD38 (partial/heterogeneous)
  NEGATIVE: CD19, CD20, CD22, CD79a, CD3, CD5, CD7, CD56, CD16, CD64, CD14, CD11b, CD15 (dim, heterogeneous - not diagnostic of monocytic differentiation), TdT, CD10

Interpretation: The aberrant blast immunophenotype is consistent with acute myeloid leukemia, myeloblastic type. The expression of CD34, CD117, CD13, CD33, HLA-DR, and MPO without lymphoid or monocytic markers supports myeloid lineage assignment. Absence of TdT and lymphoid antigens excludes acute lymphoblastic leukemia. The CD56-negative phenotype is noted. CD123 co-expression is consistent with myeloblastic AML and is not sufficient for plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (PDCN) in this context.

CYTOGENETICS (Conventional G-Banded Karyotype, bone marrow aspirate, metaphase analysis):
Number of metaphases analyzed: 20
Result: 46,XX,+8[12]/46,XX[8]

Interpretation: Clonal abnormality detected. Trisomy 8 identified as a sole cytogenetic abnormality in 12 of 20 metaphases analyzed. Eight metaphases showed a normal female karyotype, consistent with residual normal hematopoiesis. Trisomy 8 is a recurrent clonal abnormality in AML and myeloid neoplasms; as a sole abnormality, it is associated with intermediate-risk disease by ELN 2022 cytogenetic criteria (pending molecular profiling).

FISH PANEL (AML-directed FISH, bone marrow aspirate interphase nuclei, 200 cells analyzed per probe):
  t(8;21)(q22;q22) / RUNX1-RUNX1T1:     NOT DETECTED (0/200 nuclei)
  inv(16)(p13.1q22) / CBFB-MYH11:        NOT DETECTED (0/200 nuclei)
  t(15;17)(q24;q21) / PML-RARA:           NOT DETECTED (0/200 nuclei)
  11q23 (KMT2A) rearrangement:           NOT DETECTED (0/200 nuclei)
  Trisomy 8 (CEP8 probe):                DETECTED -- trisomy 8 in 58% of nuclei (consistent with clonal trisomy 8 in a subset of cells; concordant with conventional karyotype above)

MOLECULAR STUDIES (PCR/Sanger, performed on aspirate-derived DNA):
  NPM1 exon 12 mutation:       NEGATIVE (wild-type by fragment analysis)
  CEBPA mutation (bZIP/TAD):   NEGATIVE (no single or biallelic mutations detected)
  FLT3-ITD (exon 14/15):       RESULT PENDING -- send-out reference laboratory; allelic ratio to follow
  FLT3-TKD D835:               RESULT PENDING
  IDH1 R132:                   Performed, NEGATIVE
  IDH2 R140/R172:              Performed, NEGATIVE

Note: Comprehensive next-generation sequencing (NGS) myeloid mutation panel submitted concurrently to reference laboratory; results pending at time of this report.

--- DIAGNOSIS ---

Bone Marrow Core Biopsy and Aspirate, Right Posterior Iliac Crest:

ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA (AML), NOS
  - Myeloblastic type, without maturation (FAB M1 morphologic features)
  - 74% myeloblasts by aspirate 500-cell differential
  - Auer rods present
  - Myeloid immunophenotype: CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/MPO+/HLA-DR+; TdT-negative, lymphoid antigen-negative, CD56-negative
  - Core-forming leukemic blasts with 90-95% marrow cellularity and replacement of normal hematopoietic elements
  - Core biopsy: diffuse blast infiltration, sheet-like architecture
  - WHO 2022 Classification: AML, NOS (no qualifying recurrent genetic abnormality identified to date)
  - Cytogenetics: 46,XX,+8[12]/46,XX[8] -- clonal trisomy 8 as sole cytogenetic abnormality (ELN 2022 intermediate-risk cytogenetic category)
  - FISH: Negative for t(8;21), inv(16), t(15;17), and 11q23 rearrangement
  - NPM1 wild-type; CEBPA negative; IDH1/IDH2 negative
  - FLT3-ITD and FLT3-TKD status: PENDING (result to follow; ELN 2022 risk classification will be updated upon receipt)
  - NGS myeloid panel: PENDING
  - ELN 2022 Cytogenetic Risk: INTERMEDIATE (pending final molecular data for overall risk classification)

--- COMMENTS ---
The findings are diagnostic of acute myeloid leukemia. The blast percentage of 74% by aspirate differential far exceeds the 20% threshold required for AML diagnosis. Morphologic features including Auer rods, the myeloid immunophenotype, and absence of t(8;21), inv(16), and t(15;17) support the classification as AML, NOS, myeloblastic type without maturation. NPM1 and IDH1/2 are negative, and biallelic CEBPA is absent. FLT3 mutational status is critical for final ELN 2022 risk stratification and is pending; results will be communicated as an addendum upon receipt from the reference laboratory. The treating team has been notified of the preliminary diagnosis pending final molecular data.

This report has been reviewed and signed by the attending hematopathologist.

[ATTENDING HEMATOPATHOLOGIST SIGNATURE / ELECTRONIC ATTESTATION]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

--- ADDENDUM (to be issued upon receipt of FLT3 results) ---
FLT3-ITD result pending. Addendum will follow.
